source.title: Structural Damage - Crane Incident at East Breaks 160 A

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - East Breaks Block 160

event.top_event: Crane shock load due to work string falling through slips
actions_taken: Crane operator immediately stopped and slacked off to decrease load weight
P&A personnel set DU-Regular slips into Quik Pac lower bowls
Safety collar attached on top of slips prior to disconnecting elevators
Crane placed out-of-service until inspection completed
Slips changed from DU-Regular to DU-Long prior to resuming operations
event.summary: On 9-December-2019 at 0700 hours, during a blind lift of 41 joints of 3-1/2" drill pipe with BHA and mechanical cutters at East Breaks 160 A platform, the work string fell through improperly seated DU-Regular slips after the crane lowered the drill pipe approximately four feet. The tool joint contacted the single joint elevators, causing a shock load to the crane. The root cause was failure to ensure personnel properly inspected slips and bowls and operated equipment per manufacturer's instructions. Contributing factors included incomplete seating of slips, inadequate lubrication, and JSA deficiencies.

threats.name: Slips fail to properly seat in bowls
Inadequate lubrication of slips and bowls
Work string becomes caught on casing window
Improper load chart selection (static vs dynamic)
Insufficient inspection of equipment prior to use

consequences.name: Potential personnel injury from falling objects
Crane structural damage and operational downtime

controls.name: Equipment inspection prior to use
controls.barrier_role: Ensure slips, bowls, and safety collar are in proper condition before operation
controls.lod_basis: Pre-operation inspection is first line of defense to prevent equipment failure

supporting_text: The Lessee failed to ensure all equipment was properly utilized and thoroughly inspected prior to use; such as: type of slips, condition of slips, condition of bowls, and condition of safety collar.
Personnel failed to ensure slips completely seated into the bowls.
Personnel failed to ensure the slips and bowls were properly lubricated prior to use.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) with equipment maintenance steps
controls.barrier_role: Ensure JSA includes proper cleaning, greasing, and inspection procedures for slips and bowls
controls.lod_basis: JSA is foundational administrative control to ensure all personnel follow proper procedures

supporting_text: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) did not contain steps to ensure slips and bowls were properly cleaned and greased prior to use.
The Job Safety Analysis (JSA) did not address proper inspection or maintenance of slips, bowls, and safety collar utilized during the operation.

controls.name: Manufacturer's Operations/Maintenance Instruction Manual compliance
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all equipment is operated and maintained per manufacturer specifications
controls.lod_basis: Adherence to manufacturer instructions is fundamental to safe equipment operation

supporting_text: The Root Cause: BSEE discovered the lessee failed to ensure personnel were properly inspecting the slips and bowls and operating all equipment as per Manufacture's Operations/Maintenance Instruction Manual prior to use.

controls.name: Proper slip seating verification
controls.barrier_role: Verify slips are completely and securely seated in bowls before lifting
controls.lod_basis: Visual and tactile verification is second line of defense to confirm load security

supporting_text: The Lessee failed to ensure the DU Regular slips were fully set in the bowls, which then allowed the work string to fall eight to ten feet, causing a shock load to the crane.
Personnel failed to ensure slips completely seated into the bowls.

controls.name: Slip and bowl lubrication procedure
controls.barrier_role: Ensure adequate lubrication of slips and bowls to enable proper seating and grip
controls.lod_basis: Proper lubrication is essential for mechanical function of slip mechanism

supporting_text: Personnel failed to ensure the slips and bowls were properly lubricated prior to use.

controls.name: Load chart selection and verification
controls.barrier_role: Use correct dynamic load chart for offboard crane capacity assessment
controls.lod_basis: Correct load chart ensures crane is not overloaded and safe working limits are respected

supporting_text: The Lessee failed to use the correct load chart at the time of the incident. The Crane Operator was utilizing the static load chart (onboard capacity) instead of the dynamic load chart (offboard capacity).

controls.name: Load free and clear verification before lifting
controls.barrier_role: Ensure work string is free and clear of obstructions before crane lift
controls.lod_basis: Pre-lift verification prevents unexpected resistance and shock loads

supporting_text: The Lessee failed to ensure the load was free to be lifted out of the hole as per API RP 2D B.3.2.3. The crane was pulling the work string due to the Bottom Hole Assembly (BHA) being caught on the milled casing window, which caused the load to not be free and clear to be lifted.

controls.name: Safety collar inspection and maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Ensure safety collar is in proper condition with all components intact
controls.lod_basis: Safety collar provides secondary mechanical restraint for load security

supporting_text: While securing the load (work string) after the crane was shock loaded, personnel used a safety collar that had a missing die insert.

controls.name: Crane operator load monitoring and response
controls.barrier_role: Monitor load weight and respond immediately to abnormal load increases
controls.lod_basis: Real-time load monitoring and operator response can prevent or mitigate consequences

supporting_text: During the lift, the weight indicator increased to 24,000 lbs. The Crane Operator immediately stopped and slacked off to decrease the load weight.

controls.name: Crane out-of-service and inspection protocol
controls.barrier_role: Remove crane from service and conduct inspection after shock load event
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident inspection prevents continued operation of potentially damaged equipment

supporting_text: Once the crane was free of the load, it was placed back in the boom craddle and placed out-of-service until an inspection could be completed.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
